pattern: Count how many of the inner arrays each value appears in, then return, in ascending order, every value whose count equals the number of arrays.
hints: A value belongs to the answer only if it shows up in every single inner array.
Because each inner array has distinct values, tallying how many arrays contain a value tells you whether it is in all of them.
Increment a per-value counter across all arrays, then output the sorted values whose counter equals the number of arrays.

edge_inputs.description: no common element gives empty result
value missing from just one array is excluded

stress.description: several arrays drawn from a shared small universe so some values survive the intersection
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 8)
    budget = min(size, 1000)
    per = max(1, budget // k)
    universe = list(range(1, 41))
    arrays = []
    for _ in range(k):
        m = rng.randint(1, min(per, len(universe)))
        arr = rng.sample(universe, m)
        arrays.append(arr)
    return [arrays]
